title: قرار الرئيس الاعلى للجامعة رقم 75 لسنة 2001: في شأن برنامج الدراسات العليا للحصول على درجة الماجستير في ادارة الأعمال

provisions.title: ينشرهذا القرار فيالجريدةالرسميةويعمل به منتاريخصدوره.
provisions.content:
مكرر 6الى اللائحةالتنفيذيةلقانون إنشاءوتنظيمالجامعةالصادرةبالقرار رقمالسنة 1979م.المادة3ينشرهذا القرار فيالجريدةالرسميةويعمل به منتاريخصدوره.المادة4علىالجهاتالمعنيةتنفيذ هذاالقرار كلفيما يخصه.بتاريخ17/3/1421هـ.
الموافق9/6/2001م.نهيانمبارك آلنهيانالرئيسالاعلىللجامعةنشرهذا القرار فيعدد الجريدةالرسمية رقم 365ص 231./>');" />Masterof Business Administration Global Leadership and ManagementExecutive SuMBAStructure and CurriculumMBAPlanning & Implementation CommitteeProfessorRoger Roderick, DeanProfessorMasood Badri, ADVCC (faculty affairs)ProfessorGlenn R. Lowry (chair)Dr.Larry P. PleshkoDr.Taoufik ZoubeidiApril8,2001UNITEDARAB EMIRATES UNIVERSITYCollegeof Business and Economics (CBE)Office ofthe DeanCONTENTSTHEPROPOSED MBA PROGRAMVISIONFOR THE UAEU MBAINTEGRATINGTHEMES OF THE UAEU MBATHE MBAEXPERIENCE AT UAEUTABLE 1:MBA - GLOBAL LEADERSHIP AND MANAGEMENTTHE MBAEXPERIENCE AT UAEUORIENTATIONWEEKFOUNDATIONTERMEXPLORATIONTERMINTEGRATIONTERMAPPLICATIONTERMINTO THEPUBLIC FORUM AND GRADUATIONADMISSIONOF STUDENTSNEEDASSESSMENTMANAGERSSURVEYEDEMPLOYEESSURVEYED.STUDENTSSURVEYEDEXTERNALREVIEWSCOMPARISONWITH SIMILAR PROGRAMSTABLE 2:COMPARISON WITH US MBA PROGRAMS (1)TABLE 3:COMPARISON WITH UAE MBA PROGRAMSFACULTYREQUIREMENTSADMINISTRATIONOF THE PROGRAMBUDGETSTARTDATEAPPENDIXI: MBA COURSE DESCRIPTIONSFOUNDATIONTERM COURSES1. TheUAE in the Global Business Environment2.Accounting for Senior Managers3.Management of Human Resources4.Technology-Led Entrepreneurship and InnovationEXPLORATIONTERM COURSES.1.Financial Management for Corporate Strategy2.Marketing Management3.Customer Relationship Management4. GlobalInformation Technology ManagementINTEGRATIONTERM COURSES.1. GlobalOperations Management2.Managerial Economics and Globalization3.International Finance and Banking4. ActionResearch & Statistical AnalysisAPPLICATIONTERM COURSES1. FieldApplication Project2. GlobalStrategic Management3.Managing Multinational CorporationsAPPENDIXII: SURVEY DOCUMENTATIONAPPENDIXIII: EXTERNAL REVIEWSUNITEDARAB EMIRATES UNIVERSITYCollege of Business andEconomics (CBE)Officeof the DeanTHEPROPOSED MBA PROGRAMVISIONFOR THE UAEU MBAThe MBAprogram in Global Leadership and Management at the United Arab EmiratesUniversity will prepare its participants for leadership positions in the globalbusiness environment of the 21st century. Graduates will be prepared to helpguide the UAE to sustained preeminence as a commercial and economic leader inthe region and worldwide.INTEGRATINGTHEMES OF THE UAEU MBAUAE MBAswill realize this vision by integrating three themes throughout the entire MBAexperience:1.Leveraging the UAE lead in finance, banking, manufacturing, petroleum,supply chain management, and logistics;2.Exploiting the strategic geographic position of the UAE;3.Competing globally through technology-led innovation.THE MBAEXPERIENCE AT UAEUThedefining focus of the MBA experience at UAEU is leadership, innovation, andentrepreneurial creativity. Through four semesters, MBA students are guidedthrough a progression of thought and shared experience that will prepare themfor confident, competent business leadership in and beyond the UAE businessenvironment.Asstudents may enroll in the MBA from the full range of sector, academic andexperiential backgrounds found in the UAE, each entering class will worktogether over four terms with fellow students and UAEU MBA faculty members toachieve a synergistic outcome that surpasses their individual contributions. Thestructure of the UAEU MBA, emphasizing Global Leadership and Management, isillustrated in Table 1 on the following page. Detailed MBA Course Descriptionsare located in Appendix I at the end of this document./>');" />THEMBA EXPERIENCE AT UAEUThedefining focus of the MBA experience at UAEU is leadership, innovation, andentrepreneurial creativity. Through four semesters, MBA students are guidedthrough a progression of thought and shared experience that will prepare themfor confident, competent business leadership in and beyond the UAE businessenvironment.Asstudents may enroll in the MBA from the full range of sector, academic andexperiential backgrounds found in the UAE, each entering class will worktogether over four terms with fellow students and UAEU MBA faculty members toachieve a synergistic outcome that surpasses their individual contributions. Thestructure of the UAEU MBA, emphasizing Global Leadership and Management, isillustrated in Appendix A of this document.ORIENTATIONWEEKNewstudents begin their transformation to UAEU MBAs by participation in a week longorientation program prior to the commencement of classes.Theprimary focus of orientation week activities are:1.Leadership, team building, and team work2.Quantitative methods3.Information technology skills and techniquesFOUNDATIONTERMFollowingorientation, students initially enroll for the Foundation Term, during whichthey grow to know themselves, their fellows, and UAEU MBA faculty. Enteringstudents enroll in four courses designed to guide them beyond their previousacademic and professional experience into a shared appreciation of the uniquecharacteristics, opportunities, and challenges of the UAE and Global businessenvironments. Foundation Term courses are:1. TheUAE in the Global Business Environment2.Accounting for Senior Managers3.Management of Human Resources4.Technology-Led Entrepreneurship and InnovationAt theconclusion of the Foundation Term, students will complete a QualifyingExamination to assist them and the faculty to assess their interest andsuitability to continue into the more specialized work of succeeding terms. TheQualifying Examination provides an exit point for students whose work demandsand priorities may preclude their completing the entire MBA.EXPLORATIONTERMBuildingon the common foundation developed during the first term, the second terminvolves exploration of basic business subjects beyond the level ofundergraduate study or immediate work experience. Each course is presentedwithin the framework of the UAEU MBA Integrating Themes from a senior managementperspective. Exploration Term courses include:1.Financial Management for Corporate Strategy2.Marketing Management3.Customer Relationship Management4. GlobalInformation Technology ManagementINTEGRATIONTERMIn thethird term, students draw on and integrate the knowledge and perspectivesdeveloped in the previous terms in the study of the global business environmentof the present and foreseeable future. While the Foundation Term demarcated theuniverse of discourse for UAEU MBAs, the second term focused the students ontraditional business areas. The four Integration Term courses are.1.Global Operations Management2.Managerial Economics and Globalization3.International Finance and Banking4. ActionResearch & Statistical AnalysisThis termrequires integration of previous knowledge, understanding, and skills to reacha broader level of understanding of globalization and associated businessissues appropriate for senior managers in the multinational businessarena. At the conclusion of the Integration Term, students move to applicationof the insights, perspectives, and skills developed during the first three termsof the UAEU MBA program.APPLICATIONTERMThe finalterm is the Application Term, in which students orchestrate and apply theperspectives, knowledge, and skills gained in the first three terms to a realworld, empirically based action research project.Studentswill complete three courses in their final term:1. FieldApplication Project2. GlobalStrategic Management3.Managing Multinational CorporationsFollowingan initial exposure to action research in the Integration Term, students willhave numerous occasions to work with AR concepts and techniques either throughcourse work or through voluntary application of proven action researchtechniques to their professional work. In their Field Applied Project, studentsmarshal the knowledge and skill developed and articulated previously in theculminating experience of the UAEU MBA program.Undersupervision by MBA faculty and in conjunction with their employers, studentswill identify a strategic issue suitable for empirical investigation and actionrecommendation in their final MBA term.INTO THEPUBLIC FORUM AND GRADUATIONNear theconclusion of the Application Term, soon-to-be UAEU MBA graduates will presentthe results and recommendations of their Field Application Project at a PublicForum as a business case proposal to Senior Management from their employerorganization for implementation. Upon completion of all coursework,examinations, and successful presentation of the Field Application Project,students will be graduated from the MBA to take their place in the forefront ofthe United Arab Emirates business community.ADMISSIONOF STUDENTSStudentswill be selected on a competitive basis. Applications are welcome from men andwomen, UAE Nationals and expatriates residing in the UAE. Applications will beaccepted from:1.Applicants who hold a bachelor's degree from a recognized and accreditedinstitution.2.Applicants who have gained three or more years of relevant managerial orprofessional experience following graduation.3. AllMBA graduates must fulfill the requirements for graduation.a.Applicants holding a bachelor degree in business from a recognized andaccredited institution must complete a minimum of 30 credit hours beyond thebachelor's degree.b.Applicants holding a bachelor degree in a discipline other than business may beadmitted on agreeing to undertake a bridging course determined by the MBAAdmission Panel.i. TheMBA Admission Panel will review the applicant's business experience and willidentify areas in which the student may have gained equivalent experience orknowledge.ii. TheMBA Admission Panel will identify specific areas in which the applicant mustsuccessfully complete formal study to achieve the necessary background forsuccessful MBA study at the United Arab Emirates University.The MBAAdmission Panel will review applications on the basis of the followingcriteria:1.Undergraduate GPA2. TOEFLscore3.Personal interview with MBA Admission Panel4.Employer recommendations5.Admission essay from prospective studentWhen alladmission materials have been received and all steps completed, the MBAAdmission Panel will Finalize the admissions round. The panel will:1.Identify eligible and ineligible candidates.2.Eligible candidates will be ranked by the MBA Admission Panel.a. Placeswill be offered to the top ranked candidates up to the number of placesavailable in an entering cohort.b.Eligible candidates further down the list will be offered places that are nottaken up by First round candidates.c. Whenall places have been accepted and admission deposits received from candidates,remaining eligible candidates will be place on a "reserve" list.d. Anyplaces that subsequently become available will be offered to candidates on the"reserve" list.3. Adviseand thank ineligible candidates.NEEDASSESSMENTA studywas conducted to determine the market's need for the MBA and for its continueddevelopment into the future.The studyconsisted of interviews and questionnaires that solicited relevant informationfrom 79 managers, 220 employees holding the bachelor degree from 79 private andpublic establishments, each employing 200 or more staff, representing thediversity of sectors of the UAE economy from Abu Dhabi, Dubai, and SharjahEmirates. The survey also included 225 students in their senior year from anumber of colleges of the UAE University.Thefindings indicate that there is substantial demand for the MBA program asevidenced by the survey results, which are summarized below.MANAGERSSURVEYEDThemanagers of 79 firms participated in the needs analysis.Theirresponses indicated that:1. Some82% of the managers surveyed indicated that obtaining an MBA degree can helpemployees improve their organizational performance and will help the employee'sdevelopment and individual performance.2. An MBAdegree will play a significant role in development of the followingskills:a.Analy
